meta.generated: hand-seeded starter (plan 1); replaced by experiments/funding pipeline (plan 2)

nodes.name: Coefficient Giving
nodes.blurb: The largest AI-safety philanthropy — program-based funds (technical AI safety, AI governance), backed chiefly by Dustin Moskovitz and Cari Tuna.

annualFieldGivingBasis.method: sum of AI-safety-relevant grants CY2025 from the public grants database, multi-year amortized

nodes.thesis: Fund the research, institutions, and talent pipelines that make transformative AI go well; concentrated bets, program officers with real budgets.

apply.notes: RFPs per fund; general inquiries rolling

nodes.name: Survival & Flourishing Fund
nodes.blurb: S-process rounds recommending grants paid by Jaan Tallinn, Jed McCaleb, and allied donors — one of the largest x-risk funders.

annualFieldGivingBasis.method: sum of 2025 round recommendations from the public table

apply.notes: annual s-process rounds; application windows announced on the site

nodes.name: Long-Term Future Fund
nodes.blurb: EA Funds' rolling small-grants fund for AI safety and x-risk — the field's default first check for individuals and small projects.

apply.notes: apply anytime via the fund's application form; questions to longtermfuture@effectivealtruismfunds.org

nodes.name: Manifund
nodes.blurb: Regranting marketplace: named regrantors with $100k+ budgets fund AI-safety projects in public, writeups and all.

nodes.name: Schmidt Sciences
nodes.blurb: Eric and Wendy Schmidt's science philanthropy — three AI-safety programs: AI2050 fellowships (nomination-only), the $10M Science of Trustworthy AI program (27 projects incl. Bengio, Kolter), and the $10M multi-agent safety joint call.

annualFieldGivingBasis.method: AI Safety Science program commitment at launch

apply.notes: Open: 'Scaling AI Safety for a Multi-Agent World' (with Google DeepMind, CAIF, ARIA, Google.org) — Tier 1 <=$300K, Tier 2 $300K-$1M, closes Aug 8 2026. Science of Trustworthy AI 2026 RFP closed May 17 (decisions summer 2026); AI2050 is nomination-based.

sources.title: Multi-agent safety joint call — portal (tiers, deadline)

nodes.name: Macroscopic Ventures
nodes.blurb: Swiss nonprofit (formerly CERR, then Polaris Ventures) treating cooperative AI as a primary route to reducing catastrophic risk. Seeded CAIF ($15M) and CMU FOCAL ($3M); plans to give up to $100M/yr.

annualFieldGivingBasis.method: sum of grants listed on the public grants page

nodes.name: Foresight Institute
nodes.blurb: Runs focused AI-safety grant rounds (~$3M/yr) with small fast checks — plus fellowships and workshops that seed the field.

annualFieldGivingBasis.method: AI safety grants program rate per its public grant pages

apply.notes: AI for Science & Safety grants reviewed on a monthly cycle — application deadlines at the end of each month

nodes.name: Future of Life Institute
nodes.blurb: Grantmaking plus advocacy on transformative-AI risk; also appears as a payer in SFF rounds.

apply.notes: no open RFPs as of 2026-07; does not accept unsolicited requests — watch the newsletter for upcoming RFPs. Most recent fellowships closed (Technical Postdoctoral deadline 2026-01-05)

nodes.name: NSF
nodes.blurb: US federal science funding; the Safe Learning-Enabled Systems program (co-funded with Coefficient Giving) is the flagship AI-safety vehicle.

apply.notes: PESOSE (NSF 26-506) — secure open-source ecosystems incl. AI agent protocol security; Track 1 <=$300K/1yr, Tracks 2-3 <=$1.5M/2yr; deadline Sept 1 2026. SaTC 2.0 (NSF 25-515) — 'Cybersecurity and AI' explicit priority, RES track <=$1.2M/4yr, next window Sept 28 2026. SLES program continues via standing solicitations.

nodes.name: UK AISI — Alignment Project
nodes.blurb: UK government-led international coalition funding alignment research — £27M round 1 across 60+ projects, backed by Schmidt Sciences, AWS, Anthropic and others.

annualFieldGivingBasis.method: Alignment Project round 1 total £27M at 1.27 USD/GBP

nodes.name: ARIA — Safeguarded AI
nodes.blurb: UK ARIA's £59M Safeguarded AI programme — mathematically verifiable safety guarantees, £2–3.5M per team calls. Scaling Trust (~£50M) targets safe large-scale ecosystems of interacting AI agents.

annualFieldGivingBasis.method: £59M programme budget over its ~4-year life at 1.27 USD/GBP (estimate)

apply.notes: TA-level calls open and close through the programme; check current opportunities

sources.title: ARIA Scaling Trust — multi-agent coordination funding

nodes.name: OpenAI (grant programs)
nodes.blurb: Cybersecurity Grant Program — a $1M grant program across 28 projects (incl. prompt-injection work) — plus $10M in API credits via Trusted Access for Cyber.

apply.notes: Defensive cybersecurity only ($10K increments, API credits and/or direct funding). Superalignment Fast Grants ended with the team's dissolution (May 2024). Researcher Access Program: rolling, up to $1K API credits.

nodes.name: Anthropic (fellows & access)
nodes.blurb: Fellows Program cohorts (funding + mentorship, incl. AI security) and researcher compute/API access.

nodes.name: AI Safety Fund (Frontier Model Forum)
nodes.blurb: The frontier labs' pooled $10M+ safety fund — rounds in 2024 and 2025 funding independent safety research.

apply.notes: Meridian Institute (original administrator) closed June 2025; FMF self-administers. Two rounds completed (Nov 2024, Dec 2025); next round unannounced.

nodes.name: Menlo Ventures
nodes.blurb: Runs the $100M Anthology Fund with Anthropic — the most explicit VC vehicle for the AI/agent ecosystem, security startups included.

annualFieldGivingBasis.method: Anthology Fund commitment (fund total, not annual — attribution upper bound)

apply.notes: pitch anytime; Anthology targets AI-native startups

nodes.name: Sequoia Capital
nodes.blurb: Generalist tier-1 that has moved decisively into AI security — co-led Irregular's $80M Series B.

nodes.name: Insight Partners
nodes.blurb: Growth investor that led Promptfoo's $18.4M Series A — one of the clearest bets on agent-security tooling.

nodes.name: FAR.AI
nodes.blurb: Frontier-AI safety research nonprofit with $30M+ multi-funder support.

nodes.name: Redwood Research
nodes.blurb: AI control and alignment research org; long-standing Coefficient Giving grantee.

nodes.name: METR
nodes.blurb: Frontier-model autonomy and dangerous-capability evaluations.

nodes.name: Apollo Research
nodes.blurb: Deception and scheming evals for frontier models.

nodes.name: EleutherAI
nodes.blurb: Open-source AI research lab — interpretability, open models, and the infrastructure the open safety ecosystem runs on.

nodes.name: MATS
nodes.blurb: ML Alignment Theory Scholars — the field's main research-talent pipeline.

nodes.name: Center for AI Safety
nodes.blurb: Research plus field-building (compute cluster, benchmarks, statements) on societal-scale AI risk.

nodes.name: Timaeus
nodes.blurb: Developmental interpretability research org.

nodes.name: Cooperative AI Foundation
nodes.blurb: Funds cooperative AI research: multi-agent safety, coordination, and human-AI cooperation. Seeded with $15M from Macroscopic Ventures; co-funder of the $10M 'Scaling AI Safety for a Multi-Agent World' call.
nodes.thesis: Supporting the foundational scientific research needed to understand, evaluate, and control risks emerging from large-scale ecosystems of interacting AI agents, deployed by multiple actors; advancing cooperative intelligence for the benefit of all.

apply.notes: Standalone CAIF grant rounds announced periodically (2025 round closed). Open now: the joint 'Scaling AI Safety for a Multi-Agent World' call with Schmidt Sciences, Google DeepMind, ARIA, Google.org — Tier 1 <=$300K, Tier 2 $300K-$1M, closes Aug 8 2026. Annual PhD fellowship (next cycle TBA).

sources.title: CAIF research grants — status & next deadline
Scaling AI Safety for a Multi-Agent World — application portal

nodes.name: CMU FOCAL
nodes.blurb: Carnegie Mellon's Foundations of Cooperative AI Lab.

nodes.name: Irregular
nodes.blurb: AI security startup — raised an $80M Series B co-led by Sequoia and Redpoint.

nodes.name: Promptfoo
nodes.blurb: LLM red-teaming and eval tooling; $18.4M Series A led by Insight Partners — acquired by OpenAI in March 2026.

nodes.name: Austin Chen
nodes.title: Co-founder, Manifund
nodes.blurb: Runs the Manifund regranting marketplace.

nodes.name: Allison Duettmann
nodes.title: CEO, Foresight Institute
nodes.blurb: Leads Foresight's grants, fellowships, and workshops.

nodes.name: Anthony Aguirre
nodes.title: Executive Director, Future of Life Institute
nodes.blurb: Leads FLI's grantmaking and advocacy.

nodes.name: Lewis Hammond
nodes.title: Research Director
nodes.blurb: Research Director — Cooperative AI Foundation.

nodes.name: Cecilia Elena Tilli
nodes.title: Associate Director, Research and Grants
nodes.blurb: Associate Director, Research and Grants — Cooperative AI Foundation.

nodes.name: Goodfire
nodes.blurb: Mechanistic-interpretability startup (Ember platform); backed by Juniper Ventures, South Park Commons, and AI Grant.

nodes.name: Gray Swan AI
nodes.blurb: Adversarial arenas and automated jailbreak testing for frontier models and agents; Juniper Ventures portfolio.

nodes.name: Andon Labs
nodes.blurb: Autonomous-agent safety evaluations startup; Juniper Ventures portfolio.

nodes.name: Braintrust
nodes.blurb: AI evaluation infrastructure (evals, tracing, scoring) for enterprise AI teams; Greylock led its seed.

nodes.name: 7AI
nodes.blurb: Agentic security operations — autonomous SOC agents; $166M raised across a Greylock-led seed and an Index-led Series A.

nodes.name: Exaforce
nodes.blurb: Agentic SOC platform ('Exabots') — multi-model AI for detection, triage, and response; $75M Series A co-led by Khosla Ventures.

nodes.name: Surf AI
nodes.blurb: Agentic cybersecurity — agents that execute fixes rather than just flag issues; Accel led its $40M Series A.

nodes.name: Oasis Security
nodes.blurb: Non-human-identity security covering AI agents; $120M Series B joined by Accel, Sequoia, and Cyberstarts.

nodes.name: Bold Security
nodes.blurb: AI-first data-loss prevention; Bessemer-backed.

nodes.name: Haize Labs
nodes.blurb: Automated stress-testing and red-teaming of LLMs and agents ('haizing'); AI Grant Batch 4.

nodes.name: Descope
nodes.blurb: Identity and access management for customers, partners, and AI agents; Lightspeed-backed.
